QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_14

question.text: In a superannuation scheme, what portion of the accumulated corpus can be taken as commutation (lump sum)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** On retirement, the employee can take one-third of the accumulation in their superannuation account as commutation (lump sum). The balance is used to purchase an annuity. **The Trap:** One-fourth and one-half are not the correct proportions. Compare with NPS where up to 60% can be taken as lump sum. **Cross-Exam Context:** Superannuation commutation is tested in Series XVII and X-B. NPS allows 60% lump sum vs 33.33% for superannuation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Superannuation provides employer-funded retirement benefits at a lower tax cost. Payments received at retirement or death from an approved fund are exempt from tax.
